version_A.subject: Mccc's Meta spend vs. zero email
version_A.body:
Mccc Sportswear is running Meta ads to bring in customers — but without email marketing, every customer relationship ends the moment they close the tab.

We help fashion brands like yours set up Klaviyo + a loyalty program in one afternoon: cart recovery, welcome flows, and a points system for repeat seasonal buyers.

Would fixing cart abandonment first make sense as a starting point?

version_B.subject: How ActivePulse grew repeat revenue 3x without more ad spend
version_B.body:
ActivePulse (sportswear brand, similar AOV to Mccc) added email automation + a seasonal loyalty program last spring. In 90 days:
• Repeat purchase rate: +3x
• Email-attributed revenue: 28% of total
• Ad spend: unchanged

All from customers they already had. Happy to share the exact setup.

version_C.subject: What does Mccc's repeat purchase rate look like right now?
version_C.body:
Quick question — do you know what percentage of Mccc customers come back for a second seasonal purchase?

For sportswear brands without email automation, it's typically under 15%. With Klaviyo flows and a loyalty program, it's usually 35–50%.

I can model what that gap is worth for Mccc in revenue terms — takes five minutes. Want me to run it?

version_A.subject: LSD Couture's repeat gown clients — are you capturing them?
version_A.body:
LSD Couture has something most fashion brands don't — customers who come back for every major occasion. Without a loyalty program, those repeat clients have no reason to choose you over a competitor next time.

We help high-AOV fashion brands build a VIP tier connected to Klaviyo: custom reward points, birthday perks, and a referral mechanic that turns your best clients into sales reps.

Want to see what a VIP tier looks like for a couture brand? 15 minutes.

version_B.subject: How Maison Elara retained 70% of custom clients year-over-year
version_B.body:
Maison Elara (custom gown brand, similar AOV to LSD Couture) added our VIP loyalty + review display in one day. In 12 months:
• Repeat client rate: 70% (up from 31%)
• Referral-driven new clients: 22% of new revenue
• Review display added 9% to PDP conversion

Their clients feel recognized now — not just transacted with.

version_C.subject: What happens to a LSD Couture client after their gown is delivered?
version_C.body:
Genuine question — after a custom gown is delivered, what's the next touchpoint a LSD Couture client receives?

For most couture brands without a loyalty program, the answer is: nothing, until they need another gown. By then they may have forgotten you.

I can show you what a minimal VIP follow-up sequence looks like for a brand your size. 15 minutes — worth it?

version_A.subject: The Aesthetics Loft clients — are they coming back?
version_A.body:
The Aesthetics Loft has a strong client base and a clear result-driven service. The gap: without email flows, clients have no automated reason to rebook or repurchase at-home products between visits.

We help beauty clinics set up Klaviyo + a membership-style loyalty program: post-treatment sequences, product repurchase reminders, and a points system that rewards booking frequency.

Would a rebooking flow be the right first step for your practice?

version_B.body:
Glow Studio (CT beauty clinic, similar service mix to The Aesthetics Loft) launched a membership loyalty program and automated email sequence in one afternoon. In 6 months:
• Membership subscribers: 180 clients @ $65/mo
• Rebooking rate: up from 38% to 71%
• At-home product revenue: +$4,200/mo

All from clients they already had. Happy to share their setup.

version_C.subject: What's The Aesthetics Loft rebooking rate right now?
version_C.body:
Quick benchmark — what percentage of The Aesthetics Loft clients rebook within 8 weeks of their last treatment?

For beauty clinics without automated rebooking emails and a loyalty program, it's typically 35–45%. With them, it's 65–78%.

I can model what that gap is worth in monthly recurring revenue for your practice. Interested in the numbers?

version_A.body:
TheSkinBar has built one of Knoxville's most sophisticated medispa stacks — Klaviyo, Gorgias, Meta Pixel, nearly 2,000 reviews. The gap that stands out: no loyalty or membership program for clients spending $150–$1,200 per visit.

For a practice at your level, a VIP tier isn't optional — it's the difference between clients who compare prices and clients who never leave.

We integrate directly with Klaviyo and Gorgias. Want to see what a TheSkinBar membership program could look like?

version_B.body:
Illuminate MedSpa (TN, similar service mix to TheSkinBar) launched a VIP membership + referral program connected to Klaviyo. In 8 months:
• Monthly membership revenue: $45,200
• Referral-driven new clients: 34% of all new bookings

One afternoon of setup. Happy to walk you through their exact tier structure.

version_C.subject: What percentage of TheSkinBar clients return for a second treatment?
version_C.body:
Quick benchmark — for a medispa at TheSkinBar's level, what's your 6-month client return rate?

For high-end medspas without a formal loyalty program, it's typically 42–55%. With a VIP membership tier, it's 72–85%.

On $1,200 treatments, that gap is enormous in revenue terms. I can model it for TheSkinBar specifically — want me to run the numbers?

version_A.subject: Obroi's family audience is un-segmented
version_A.body:
Obroi serves women, men, and kids — one of the broadest fashion audiences on Shopify. The problem: without email segmentation, every customer gets the same message (or no message at all).

We help multi-category fashion brands set up Klaviyo with family-based segmentation: separate flows for women's, men's, and kids' buyers, plus a unified loyalty program where family points accumulate across all purchases.

Would back-to-school season be the right time to launch this?

version_B.subject: How FamilyThreads 2x'd AOV with family loyalty points
version_B.body:
FamilyThreads (multi-category fashion, similar structure to Obroi) launched a unified family loyalty program — points earned on kids, women's, and men's purchases all stack.

In 90 days:
• Average order value: +2.1x (families shop across more categories per visit)
• Repeat purchase rate: +88%
• Email open rates: 41% (segmented vs. 12% generic)

Happy to share their segmentation structure.

version_C.subject: What's Obroi's repeat purchase rate across customer segments?
version_C.body:
Quick question — do you know whether your women's buyers, men's buyers, and kids' buyers have different repeat purchase rates?

For multi-category fashion brands without email segmentation, all three groups typically underperform because they get generic messaging. Segmented brands see 2–3x higher engagement per category.

I can show you what a segmented setup looks like for Obroi. 15 minutes?

Beyond the Hot Room has built a strong clean beauty audience — 730 reviews, a wellness-focused catalog, active social. The gap: Mailchimp can't trigger on Shopify events, so every abandoned cart, browse session, and post-purchase moment is silent.

We help clean beauty retailers make the switch to Klaviyo + loyalty in a single afternoon — list migration included, no data loss.

Is fixing cart abandonment on your roadmap for Q1?

version_B.body:
PureRoot (clean beauty retailer, similar AOV and values to Beyond the Hot Room) switched from Mailchimp to our Klaviyo + loyalty stack in one day.

In 90 days:
• Email-attributed revenue: 4.2x
• Cart recovery: $8,400 recovered in first 60 days
• Repeat purchase rate: +61%

Subscriber list moved over completely. Happy to share their migration process.

version_C.subject: How many Beyond the Hot Room carts go unrecovered each week?
version_C.body:

For Shopify stores on Mailchimp, the answer is typically all of them — Mailchimp can't trigger on Shopify cart events without expensive workarounds.

I can show you what that lost revenue looks like in dollar terms for your store. No pitch — just numbers. Interested?

We help niche beauty brands activate their community: athlete-themed points, referral mechanics for gym/running communities, and a review display that shows your social proof on every product page.

Want to see what a Yendra loyalty program could look like? 15 minutes.

In 6 months:

For beauty brands without a loyalty or subscription mechanic, it's typically 22–30%. With one, athlete-targeted brands typically see 55–70%.

Want to see what this looks like for Troya's catalog? 15 minutes.

Would early access emails for limited drops be the right first step?

version_A.subject: Wing+Weft's gifting buyers — are they referring?
version_A.body:
Wing+Weft Gloves has a product that sells itself at gift-giving moments — custom, fashion-forward, and giftable. The gap: without a referral mechanic, every buyer who gifts a pair of gloves exposes Wing+Weft to a new customer you can't capture.

We add a gift-specific referral program to Klaviyo: the gift recipient gets a discount code, the buyer gets loyalty points, and Wing+Weft gets a new customer.

Would a pre-holiday referral setup make sense this season?

The product did the heavy lifting — the mechanic just captured it. Happy to share their setup.

version_C.body:

For most fashion brands without a gift-to-referral mechanic, the answer is: nothing. They can't find the brand, or they forget.

I can show you what a gift-card follow-up + referral sequence looks like for Wing+Weft. 15 minutes?
